scheme_name: Financial Assistance to SC Students Studying in Medical, Engineering and Diploma Course for Purchasing Educational Instrument
page_title: Financial Assistance to SC Students Studying in Medical, Engineering and Diploma Course for Purchasing Educational Instrument

details: The “Financial Assistance to SC Students Studying in Medical, Engineering and Diploma Course for Purchasing Educational Instrument” scheme is administered by the Director, Scheduled Caste Welfare, Government of Gujarat. Under the scheme, financial assistance is provided to Scheduled Caste students who are studying Medical, Engineering, and Diploma Courses for purchasing educational equipment/instruments, etc.
benefits: The assistance shall be given in the first year only.
eligibility: The student should belong to the Scheduled Caste of Gujarat.
The student should be studying in Medical, Engineering, or Diploma courses.
The annual family income of the student should be up to ₹6,00,000/-.

online: The applicant may visit the Digital Gujarat Portal:
On the home page, click on ‘Register’ and the applicant will be redirected to the registration form page.
Enter your Mobile Number, Email, Set Password, and then click on ‘Save’.
After successful registration, the applicant can login through his/her User Name (Mobile No./Email ID) and Password.
Now, click on ‘My Profile’ to update your profile and fill in all the mandatory information.
After filling profile details, click on ‘Update Profile’. The profile will be updated after entering the verification Code (OTP).
Now under services, select the ‘Scholarship Services’ and then click on ‘SC Scheme’.
Select the Financial Year in which you want to apply for and then click on ‘Change Financial Year’.
Select the scheme, read the interaction carefully and then click on ‘Continue to Service’.
Fill in the application form completely and upload all the relevant documents.
Now, click on ‘Save as Draft’ and agree with the terms & conditions, and submit the application form by clicking on ‘Confirm & Final Submit’.
Photo uploading for all applicants is mandatory.
The application will be considered complete only when all sections are completed.

Only one application per applicant will be acceptable for Instrumental Assistance. If any student submits more than one application either from a single institute or from multiple institutions his/her applications will be rejected by the competent authority and no request from any side will be entertained.
In case of any wrong/misleading information provided in the application shall lead to rejection of the application by Department Authorities.
All required documents must be carefully scanned as per the instructions given.
The student has to fill his/her application carefully as there is no provision for institution change once the application is submitted successfully.
: The unreadable uploaded documents will allow the competent authority to reject student applications.
Direct Individual submission of this application form will not be accepted in case of students studying inside the state.

documents_required: 3. Caste Certificate from the Competent Authorities
4. Income Certificate (Competent Authority) (Form No. 16 required for Govt. Employee)
6. Break Affidavit (If Break Gap is more than One Year)
8. First Page of Bank Passbook/Cancelled Cheque
10. Death Certificate of Father (If Father not Alive)
The Validity of the Income Certificate is 3 years. It is advised to check the Income Certificate issue date before uploading the document.

faqs.question: What type of financial assistance is provided under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Scheduled Caste students studying Medical, Engineering, and Diploma courses receive financial assistance for purchasing educational equipment.

faqs.question: What are the scholarship rates per student?
faqs.answer: Medical students receive ₹10,000/-, Engineering students receive ₹8000/-, and Diploma students receive ₹3000/-.

faqs.question: Are there any conditions regarding the distribution of financial assistance?
faqs.answer: Yes, the assistance is given only in the first year of the course.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Students belonging to the Scheduled Caste community of Gujarat are eligible.

faqs.question: What courses are covered under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Medical, Engineering, Degree, and Diploma courses are covered.

faqs.question: What is the maximum annual family income limit for eligibility?
faqs.answer: The student's annual family income should not exceed ₹6,00,000/-.

faqs.question: How can one apply for the benefits of this scheme?
faqs.answer: Applicants can apply through the Digital Gujarat Portal at https://www.digitalgujarat.gov.in/.
